analysis.reasoning: Core message: A U.S. Assistant Secretary states the U.S. will press involved countries that a peaceful resolution is the only viable path to end Libya’s conflict, linking U.S. engagement to peace. Evaluation: US1 (honorable tradition) – Not invoked; no reference to history or legacy. US2 (admirable society) – Not relevant; no mention of society, diversity, or civil actors. US3 (hostile enemies) – No adversary is highlighted or condemned. US4 (force for good) – Yes; the U.S. positions itself as working toward peace and conflict resolution in Libya, aligning with support for peace and stability. US5 (great international ally) – Not explicit; no praise of allies or assurances to a specific partner. US6 (at the forefront) – Implicit leadership could be inferred, but the tweet focuses on advocacy for peace rather than asserting U.S. leadership status; insufficient to claim this narrative. Final determination: US4.
